page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server optimized for high performance computing workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs, maintaining a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The memory configuration consists of 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the hpc7a.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, achieving over 7,105 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 695 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% on Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests. The system also excels in database operations and Redis SET operations, both scoring in the top 10%.

The absence of local storage and GPU accelerators represents a clear architectural tradeoff, requiring external storage solutions and limiting its applicability for GPU-dependent deep learning. However, the high density of dedicated physical cores combined with massive DDR5 memory bandwidth offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound and compute-heavy parallel workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale scientific simulations, software compilation, molecular modeling, and complex database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is an x86_64 high performance computing server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on external storage but delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Benchmarks place its multi-core CPU and memory read/write speeds in the top 10% globally. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-bound parallel workloads, software compilation, and large-scale database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high performance computing server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is an x86_64 server with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute node featuring massive DDR5 memory bandwidth and high-speed networking for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with 16 GB per core
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and parallel computing
